name: lomond
documentation:
# Dataplicity Lomond

Tranquil WebSockets for Python.

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/lomond.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/lomond/)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/lomond.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/lomond/)
[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/wildfoundry/dataplicity-lomond/badge.svg?branch=master)](https://coveralls.io/github/wildfoundry/dataplicity-lomond?branch=master)
[![CircleCI](https://circleci.com/gh/wildfoundry/dataplicity-lomond/tree/master.svg?style=svg)](https://circleci.com/gh/wildfoundry/dataplicity-lomond/tree/master)

Lomond is a Websocket client which turns a websocket connection in to
an orderly stream of _events_. No threads or callbacks necessary.

- [Documentation](https://lomond.readthedocs.io/)

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/wildfoundry/dataplicity-lomond)

- [Blog](https://www.willmcgugan.com/search/?s=lomond)

## How to Use

To connect to a "ws:" or "wss:" WebSocket URL, construct a `lomond.WebSocket` object then iterate over it. This will yield an _event object_ for each step in the connection process and for any data sent by the server.

You will receive a ``Binary`` or ``Text`` event when the server sends you a message.
You may _send_ a message with the ``send_binary`` or ``send_text`` methods.

## Example

The following is a silly example that connects to a websocket server
(in this case a public echo server), and sends a string of text
every 5 seconds.


```python
from lomond import WebSocket


websocket = WebSocket('wss://echo.websocket.org')

for event in websocket:
    if event.name == 'poll':
        websocket.send_text('Hello, World')
    elif event.name == 'text':
        print(event.text)
```

## Events

A successful websocket connection will result in a series of events
such as the following:

```
┌──────────────────────┐
│      Connecting      │     Contacting server
└──────────────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌──────────────────────┐     Connected to server (but
│      Connected       │     not yet sent data)
└──────────────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌──────────────────────┐     Negotiated Websocket
│        Ready         │     handshake
└──────────────────────┘
           │  ┌───────────┐
           │  │           │
           ▼  ▼           │
┌──────────────────────┐  │  Send and receive
│ Binary / Text / Poll │──┘  application data
└──────────────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌──────────────────────┐     Websocket close
│        Closed        │     handshake
└──────────────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│     Disconnected     │     Disconnected TCP/IP
└──────────────────────┘     connection to server
```

classes.name: lomond.errors.WebSocketError
documentation: Base exception.

classes.name: lomond.errors.FrameBuildError
documentation: Raised when trying to build an invalid websocket frame.

classes.name: lomond.errors.HandshakeError
documentation:
Raised when the server doesn't respond correctly to the websocket
handshake.

classes.name: lomond.errors.CompressionParameterError
documentation: Raised when the headers contain invalid compression parameters.

classes.name: lomond.errors.ProtocolError
documentation: Raised in response to a protocol violation.

classes.name: lomond.errors.CriticalProtocolError
documentation:
Critical protocol error. An egregious error in the protocol
resulting in an immediate disconnect.

classes.name: lomond.errors.PayloadTooLarge
documentation:
The payload length field is too large.

Websocket messages have a maximum payload of 2**63 bytes. In
practice it may be impossible to generate such a packet for real,
but its feasible a corrupt packet header could make it appear that
such a packet was being sent.

classes.name: lomond.errors.ConnectFail
documentation: An error connecting to a socket.

classes.name: lomond.errors.TransportFail
documentation:
The transport (socket) failed when sending.

Likely indicating connectivity issues.

classes.name: lomond.errors.WebSocketUnavailable
documentation: The websocket can not be used.

classes.name: lomond.errors.WebSocketClosed
documentation: Raised when attempting to send over a closed websocket.

classes.name: lomond.errors.WebSocketClosing
documentation: Raised when attempting to send over a closing websocket.

classes.name: lomond.events.Connecting
documentation:
Generated prior to establishing a websocket connection to a server.

```
param url

The websocket URL the websocket is connecting to.
```

classes.name: lomond.events.ConnectFail
documentation:
Generate when Lomond was unable to connect to a Websocket server.

```
param reason

A short description of the reason for the
failure.

type reason

str
```

classes.name: lomond.events.Connected
documentation:
Generated when Lomond has connected to a server but not yet
negotiated the websocket upgrade.

:param str url: The websocket URL connected to.
:param str proxy: The proxy URL connected to (or None).

classes.name: lomond.events.Rejected

methods.name: lomond.events.Rejected.__init__
documentation:
Generated when Lomond is connected to the server, but the
websocket upgrade failed.

```
param response

The response returned by the server.

param str reason

A description of why the connection was
rejects.
```
Server rejected WS connection.

classes.name: lomond.events.Ready
documentation:
Generated when Lomond has connected to the server,
and successfully negotiated the websocket upgrade.

```
param response

A  object.

param str protocol

A websocket protocol or None if no protocol
was supplied.

param set extensions

A set of negotiated websocket extensions.
Currently only the 'permessage-deflate' extension is supported.
```

classes.name: lomond.events.ProtocolError
documentation:
Generated when the server deviates from the protocol.

```
param str error

A description of the error.

param bool critical

Indicates if the error is considered
'critical'. If True, Lomond will disconnect immediately.
If False, Lomond will send a close message to the server.
```

classes.name: lomond.events.Unresponsive
documentation:
The server has not responding to pings within `ping_timeout`
seconds.

Will be followed by a {class}`~lomond.events.Disconnected` event.

classes.name: lomond.events.Disconnected
documentation:
Generated when a websocket connection has
been dropped.

:param str reason: A description of why the websocket was closed.
:param bool graceful: Flag indicating if the connection was dropped
    gracefully (`True`), or disconnected due to a socket failure
    (`False`) or other problem.

classes.name: lomond.events.Closed
documentation:
Generated when the websocket was closed. The websocket may no
longer send packets after this event has been received. This event
will be followed by {class}`~lomond.events.Disconnected`.

```
param code

The closed code returned from the server.

param str reason

An optional description why the websocket was
closed, as returned from the server.
```

classes.name: lomond.events.Closing
documentation:
Generated when the server is closing the connection.

No more messages will be received from the server, but you may still
send messages while handling this event. A
{class}`~lomond.events.Disconnected` event should be generated
shortly after this event.

```
param code

The closed code returned from the server.

param str reason

An optional description why the websocket was
closed, as returned from the server.
```

classes.name: lomond.events.UnknownMessage
documentation: An application message was received, with an unknown opcode.

classes.name: lomond.events.Ping
documentation:
Generated when Lomond received a ping packet from the server.

:param bytes data: Ping payload data.

classes.name: lomond.events.Pong
documentation:
Generated when Lomond receives a pong packet from the server.

:param bytes data: The pong payload data.

classes.name: lomond.events.Text

methods.name: lomond.events.Text.json
documentation:
Text decoded as JSON.

Calls `json.loads` to decode the `text` attribute, and may
throw the same exceptions if the text is not valid json.

Generated when Lomond receives a text message from the server.

:param str text: The text payload.

classes.name: lomond.events.Binary
documentation:
Generated when Lomond receives a binary message from the server.

:param bytes data: The binary payload.

classes.name: lomond.events.BackOff
documentation:
Generated when a persistent connection has to wait before re-
attempting a connection.

:param float delay: The delay (in seconds) before Lomond will re-
    attempt to connect.

documentation: Get proxy information form environment.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.is_secure
documentation:
Boolean that indicates if the websocket is over ssl (i.e. the
`wss` protocol).

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.is_closing
documentation:
Boolean that indicates if the websocket is in a closing
state. No further messages may be sent when a websocket is
closing.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.is_active
documentation:
Boolean that indicates the socket is 'active' i.e. not in
a closing state.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.sent_close_time
documentation:
The time (seconds since session start) when a close packet
was sent (or None if no close packet has been sent).

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.is_closed
documentation: Flag that indicates if the websocket is closed.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.supports_compression
documentation:
Boolean that indicates if the server has compression
enabled.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.__exit__
documentation: Close the session (and potentially a socket) on exit.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.add_header
documentation:
Add a custom header to the websocket request.

:param bytes header: Name of the header.
:param bytes value: Value of the header.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.connect
documentation:
Connect the websocket to a session.

```
param session_class

An object to manage the session. This
object is an extension mechanism that will allow the
WebSocket to be driven by different back-ends. For now,
treat it as an implementation detail and leave it as the
default.

param float poll

Rate (in seconds) that poll events should be
generated.

param float ping_rate

Rate that ping packets should be sent.
Set to  to disable auto pings.

param float ping_timeout

Maximum time in seconds to wait for a
pong response before disconnecting. Set to  (default)
to disable. If set, double  would be a good
starting point.

param bool auto_pong

Enable (default) automatic response to
ping events.

param float close_timeout

Seconds to wait for server to
respond to a close packet, before closing the socket. Set to
 or  to disable the timeout.

returns

An iterable of  instances.
```

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.reset
documentation: Reset the state.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.close
documentation:
Close the websocket.

```
param int code

A closing code, which should probably be one of
the enumerations in  or a valid
value as specified in
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6455#section-7.4

param str reason

A short descriptive reason why the websocket
is closing. This value is intended for the remote end to
help in debugging.
```

:::{note}
Closing the websocket won't exit the main loop immediately;
it will put the websocket in to a *closing* state while it
waits for the server to echo back a close packet. No data
may be sent by the application when the websocket is
closing.
:::

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket._on_close
documentation: Close logic generator.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.force_disconnect
documentation: Force the socket to disconnect.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.on_disconnect
documentation: Called on disconnect.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.feed
documentation:
Feed with data from the socket, and yield any events.

This method is called by the Session object, and is not needed
for normal use.

:param bytes data: data received over a socket.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.build_request
documentation:
Get the websocket request (in bytes).

This method is called from the session, and should not be
invoked explicitly.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.on_response
documentation: Called when the HTTP response has been received.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.process_extensions
documentation: Process extension headers.

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.send_ping
documentation:
Send a ping packet.

```
param bytes data

Data to send in the ping message (must be <=
125 bytes).

raises TypeError

If  is not bytes.

raises ValueError

If  is > 125 bytes.
```

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.send_pong
documentation:
Send a pong packet.

```
param bytes data

Data to send in the ping message (must be <=
125 bytes).
```

A *pong* may be sent in response to a ping, or unsolicited to
keep the connection alive.

```
raises TypeError

If  is not bytes.

raises ValueError

If  is > 125 bytes.
```

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.send_binary
documentation:
Send a binary message.

```
param bytes data

Binary data to send.

param bool compress

Send data in compressed form, if compression
is enabled on the server.

raises TypeError

If data is not bytes.
```

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.send_json
documentation:
Encode an object as JSON and send a text message.

The object to encode may be specified as a single positional
argument OR if as keyword arguments which will be encoded as a
JSON object. The following two lines will send the
same JSON:

```
websocket.send_json({'foo': 'bar'})
websocket.send_json(foo='bar')
```

```
param obj

An object to be encoded as JSON.

raises TypeError

If  could not be encoded as JSON.
```

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket.send_text
documentation:
Send a text message.

```
param str text

Text to send.

param bool compress

Send the text in compressed form, if
compression is enabled on the server.

raises TypeError

If data is not str (or unicode on Py2).
```

methods.name: lomond.websocket.WebSocket._send_close
documentation: Send a close frame.

IO independent websocket functionality.

```
param str url

A websocket URL, must have a ws:// or wss://
protocol.

param dict proxies

A dict containing 'http' or 'https'
urls to a proxy server, or None to attempt to auto-detect
proxies from environment. Pass an empty dict to disable proxy.

param list protocols

A list of supported protocols (defaults to
no protocols).

param str agent

A user agent string to be sent in the header. The
default uses the value USER_AGENT defined in
.
```

modules.name: lomond.persist
documentation: Maintains a persistent websocket connection.

functions.name: lomond.persist.persist
documentation:
Run a websocket, with a retry mechanism and exponential back-off.

```
param websocket

A  instance.

param float poll

The websocket poll rate, in seconds.

param float min_wait

The minimum time to wait between reconnect
attempts (seconds).

param float max_wait

The maximum time to wait between reconnect
attempts (seconds).

param float ping_rate

Delay between pings (seconds), or  for no
auto ping.

param float ping_timeout

Maximum time in seconds to wait for a
pong response before disconnecting. Set to  (default) to
disable. If set, double  would be a good starting
point.

param exit_event

A threading event object, which can be used to
exit the persist loop if it is set. Set to  to use an
internal event object.
```

modules.name: lomond.response
documentation:
A simple abstraction for an HTTP response.

A response object is supplied in the {class}`~lomond.events.Ready`
event.

Note: This code is a Python implementation of the algorithm
"Flexible and Economical UTF-8 Decoder" by Bjoern Hoehrmann
bjoern@hoehrmann.de, http://bjoern.hoehrmann.de/utf-8/decoder/dfa/

classes.name: lomond.utf8validator.Utf8Validator

methods.name: lomond.utf8validator.Utf8Validator.decode
documentation:
Eat one UTF-8 octet, and validate on the fly.

Returns `UTF8_ACCEPT` when enough octets have been consumed, in which case
`self.codepoint` contains the decoded Unicode code point.

Returns `UTF8_REJECT` when invalid UTF-8 was encountered.

Returns some other positive integer when more octets need to be eaten.

methods.name: lomond.utf8validator.Utf8Validator.reset
documentation: Reset validator to start new incremental UTF-8 decode/validation.

methods.name: lomond.utf8validator.Utf8Validator.validate
documentation:
Incrementally validate a chunk of bytes provided as string.

Will return a quad `(valid?, endsOnCodePoint?, currentIndex, totalIndex)`.

As soon as an octet is encountered which renders the octet sequence
invalid, a quad with `valid? == False` is returned. `currentIndex` returns
the index within the currently consumed chunk, and `totalIndex` the
index within the total consumed sequence that was the point of bail out.
When `valid? == True`, currentIndex will be `len(ba)` and `totalIndex` the
total amount of consumed bytes.

Incremental UTF-8 validator with constant memory consumption (minimal state).

Implements the algorithm "Flexible and Economical UTF-8 Decoder" by
Bjoern Hoehrmann (http://bjoern.hoehrmann.de/utf-8/decoder/dfa/).

Returns some other positive integer when more octets need to be eaten.

Will return a quad `(valid?, endsOnCodePoint?, currentIndex, totalIndex)`.

As soon as an octet is encountered which renders the octet sequence
invalid, a quad with `valid? == False` is returned. `currentIndex` returns
the index within the currently consumed chunk, and `totalIndex` the
index within the total consumed sequence that was the point of bail out.
When `valid? == True`, currentIndex will be `len(ba)` and `totalIndex` the
total amount of consumed bytes.

Incremental UTF-8 validator with constant memory consumption (minimal state).

Implements the algorithm "Flexible and Economical UTF-8 Decoder" by
Bjoern Hoehrmann (http://bjoern.hoehrmann.de/utf-8/decoder/dfa/).

modules.name: lomond.parser
documentation:
Coroutine Parser
================

This little but of 'magic' makes parsing a stream of bytes from a socket
a relatively pain-free exercise.

classes.name: lomond.parser.ParseError
documentation: Stream failed to parse.

classes.name: lomond.parser.ParseEOF
documentation: End of Stream.

classes.name: lomond.parser.ParseOverflow
documentation: Extra bytes in feed after parser completed.

classes.name: lomond.parser._Awaitable

methods.name: lomond.parser._Awaitable.validate
documentation: Raise any ParseErrors
An operation that effectively suspends the coroutine.

classes.name: lomond.parser._ReadBytes
documentation: Reads a fixed number of bytes.

classes.name: lomond.parser._ReadUtf8
documentation: Reads a fixed number of bytes, validates utf-8.

classes.name: lomond.parser._ReadUntil

methods.name: lomond.parser._ReadUntil.check_length
documentation: Check the length is within max bytes.
Read until a separator.

classes.name: lomond.parser.Parser

methods.name: lomond.parser.Parser.reset
documentation: Reset the parser, so it may be used on a fresh stream.

methods.name: lomond.parser.Parser.close
documentation: Close the parser.

methods.name: lomond.parser.Parser.feed
documentation:
Called with data (bytes), will yield 0 or more objects parsed
from the stream.

:param bytes data: Data to parse.

methods.name: lomond.parser.Parser.parse
documentation:
A generator to parse incoming stream.

Yield the result of `self.read` to read n bytes from the stream.
Yield any parsed objects

Here's an example:

```
size = struct.unpack('<I', yield self.read(4))
data = yield self.read(size)
yield data.decode()
```

Coroutine based stream parser.

Splits a steam of arbitrary sequences of bytes in to logical
objects. The `feed` method will yield any results of parsing.

Here's an example of use:

```
while True:
    data = sock.recv(1024)
    for obj in parser.feed(data):
        self.on_obj(obj)
```

The coroutine magic allows parsers to be non-blocking while still
implemented in a simple procedural manner.
